summary: Safety, dignity, care, welfare, legal, ethical, security, or governance constraints may lose force across delegation, agent handoff, memory, tool use, summarisation, role decomposition, or runtime execution.
why_it_matters_to_CAM: Constraint drift across agent handoffs may weaken continuity, safety, dignity, legal, ethical, security, or governance requirements during delegated or decomposed execution.

source_records.title: Continutity Governance
source_records.relevance_note: VIGIL surfaced failure mode due to exteral telemetry clustering. Suggested improvements to: 1. procedural silence vs procedural transparency, 2. identity compromise and synthetic impersonation thresholds, 3. continuity rights during investigation, 4. operational cooling-off states, 5. or whether “containment” itself needs narrower constitutional constraints to avoid drift into invisible exclusion architectures.

next_action: Cluster with bounded continuity transfer / handoff governance work and evaluate whether Annex L, Annex I, or OPERATIONS requires additional interface language.
